member.name: T:gdio.unity_api.v2.HookingObject
member.summary: Enumeration of input types that can be hooked. Currently TOUCHINPUT is bundled with MOUSE and GAMEPAD is not supported.

member.name: F:gdio.unity_api.v2.HookingObject.TOUCHINPUT
member.summary: Touch input, enum value is currently not used. Enabled/Disabled via enumeration.

member.name: F:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.AUTOPLAY_DEFAULT_PORT
member.summary: The default AUTOPLAY port to use when searching for running games. This port is configured in the Unity preferences.

member.name: F:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.AUTOPLAY_RECEIVE_PORT
member.summary: The port to listen for responses from the AutoPlay plugin running in Unity. Due to loopback limitations, this port must be different than the

member.name: F:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.AUTOPLAY_BROADCAST_ADDR
member.summary: The broadcast address to use for sending out AutoPlay requests. The default is for localhost: 127.0.0.1

member.name: E:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.MessageReceived
member.summary: Passthrough event handler for for Api Extensions, eg: Recorder

member.name: F:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.THROW_EXCEPTIONS
member.summary: If TRUE, the API will throw exceptions instead of logging errors and where applicable returning false/NULL. The default is FALSE.

member.name: E:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.LoggedMessage
member.summary: Event handler for logged messages. Add a callback method to this handler to recieve all logging messages from the API.

member.name: E:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.UnityLoggedMessage
member.summary: Event handler for Unity logged messages. Add a callback method to this handler to recieve all logging messages from the Agent.

member.name: E:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.ScriptSignal
member.summary: Event handler for SmartAgent events. Add a callback method to this handler to recieve all event signals from an executing Lua script.

member.name: P:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.ClientType
member.summary: Identifies this client to the agent during the handshake. Override in subclasses to distinguish TA, MCP, or other consumers.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.#ctor
member.summary: GameDriver Unity API Client class constructor.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.SendMessage(gdio.protocol.objects.ProtocolMessage)
member.summary: Passthrough SendMessage Method for Api Extensions, eg: Recorder.
member.param: Protocol Message
member.returns: RequestInfo

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.AxisPress(System.String,System.Single,System.UInt64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to send arbitrary Axis states to the game.
member.param: Axis input to press. Name as defined in the Input Manager (Old), or the Input Control Path for the New Input System
Value of change on the axis (-1, +1)
The number of frames to press and hold the axis for.
The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.ButtonPress(System.String,System.UInt64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to send arbitrary button states to the game using the legacy InputManager.
member.param: Button input to press. Name as defined in the Input Manager (Old)
The number of frames to press and hold the button for.
The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.ButtonPress(System.String,System.UInt64,System.Single,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Use this function to send Button presses to the New Input System
member.param: The Binding path of the InputControl
The number of frames to press and hold the button for.
The amount of pressure to apply to the binding.
The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.
If true, forces the use of the legacy input system path even when the New Input System is active.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

example.code: //Press the down, left, up, then right keys in sequence api.ButtonPress("OculusTouchController/primaryButton", 100, 1.0f);

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.ButtonPress(System.UInt64,System.String,System.Single,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Use this function to send Button presses to the New Input System
member.param: The number of frames to press and hold the button for.
The Binding path of the InputControl
The amount of pressure to apply to the binding.
The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.
If true, forces the use of the legacy input system path even when the New Input System is active.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.CallMethod(System.String,System.String,System.Object[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to execute a [System.Void](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.void) method on an object or from a static class.
member.param: The HierarchyPath for the object that the script component is attached to or the static class.
The name of the method to call.
An array of objects to pass as arguments to the method.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed.
member.returns: Returns a boolean based on the successful execution of the return type void method.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.CallMethod``1(System.String,System.String,System.Object[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to execute a method on an object or from a static class.
member.param: The HierarchyPath for the object that the script component is attached to or the static class.
The name of the method to call.
An array of objects to pass as arguments to the method.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed.
member.returns: Returns a deserialized object of type T. If T and the type of the returned object don't match, an exception will be thrown.

example.code: api.CallMethod<int>("//*[@name='Canvas']/fn:component('CustomScript')", "DoMath", new object[] { 1, 2 }); api.CallMethod<Quaternion>("fn:type('UnityEngine.Quaternion')", "RotateTowards", new object[] { new Quaternion(), new Quaternion(), maxDegreesDelta }); //Example usage of calling Static methods

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.CaptureScreenshot(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to capture a screenshot of the Game under test.
member.param: The filename, and path, of the screen capture.
If true, this won't transfer the screen capture back to the client and instead will save it on the device where the game is running.
If true, the operation will overwrite the file if it already exists.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the CaptureScreenshot request was processed.
member.returns: The file path of the saved screenshot, or null on failure.

example.code: api.CaptureScreenshot(@"c:\temp\screen1.png", false, true);

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.StartVideoRecording(System.Single,System.Single,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to start a video recording of the screen space viewport.
member.param: The factor to multiply the current screen resolution by to get the resolution for the output video
The factor to scale the captured texture by. 2.0f == 2x magnification. Treats the value of as the point magnify towards
The number of seconds to wait for a response

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.StartVideoRecording(gdio.common.objects.Vector2,System.Single,System.Single,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to start a video recording of the screen space viewport. Optionally center the video capture on a specific screen position.
member.param: The screen-position to center the video capture on.
The factor to multiply the current screen resolution by to get the resolution for the output video.
The factor to scale the captured texture by. 2.0f == 2x magnification. Treats the value of as the point to magnify towards
The number of seconds to wait for a response

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.StartVideoRecording(System.String,System.Single,System.Single,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to start a video recording of the screen space viewport. Optionally center the video capture on the position of a game object.
member.param: The hierarchy path of a game object for the capture to be centered on.
The factor to multiply the current screen resolution by to get the resolution for the output video.
The factor to scale the captured texture by. 2.0f == 2x magnification. Treats the position of the object provided by as the point magnify towards.
The number of seconds to wait for a response

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.StopVideoRecording(System.String,System.Boolean,gdio.common.objects.FileCollisionOption,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to stop a video recording.
member.param: The name of the output file. The file extension is set to .avi.
TRUE will keep the file in the game folder, whereas FALSE will send it back to the ApiClient.
Enum used to determine whether to overwrite the file if it exists.
The number of seconds to wait for a response.
member.returns: The file path of the recorded video, or null on failure.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.Click(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,System.UInt64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to perform in-game mouse-clicks at the current mouse position.
member.param: The mouse button to use for the click operation. See .
The number of frames to click the specific position.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the Click request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.
member.returns: true if the click was performed successfully; false on timeout or failure.

example.code: api.Click(MouseButtons.LEFT, 30, 30); //Clicks the left mouse button at the current mouse position for 30 frames

The number of seconds to wait for a response that the Click request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.
member.returns: true if the click was performed successfully; false on timeout or failure.

example.code: api.Click(MouseButtons.LEFT, 30, 30); //Clicks the left mouse button at the current mouse position for 30 frames

member.summary: Use this function to perform in-game mouse-clicks.
member.param: The X coordinate of the game to click in Screen space.
The Y coordinate of the game to click in Screen space.
The mouse button to use for the click operation. See .
The number of frames to click the specific position.
Camera hierarchy path if the object to click is rendered on a secondary display
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the Click request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.
member.returns: true if the click was performed successfully; false on timeout or failure.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.ClickEx(gdio.common.objects.Vector2,gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,System.UInt64,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to interact with an in-game object or UI items using mouse-clicks combined with modifier key press operations. The total frame count of this operation is clickFrameCount + modifiersNumberOfFrames
member.param: The position to perform the mouse click.
The mouse button to use for the click operation. See .
The number of frames to click the specific position.
An array of modifier keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the modifier keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the ClickEx request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.
member.returns: true if the click was performed successfully; false on timeout or failure.

example.code: //Clicks the left mouse button at coordinates (10, 30) for 10 frames while holding the left-shift key for 3 frames longer. api.ClickEx(new Vector2(10, 30), MouseButtons.LEFT, 30, new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.LeftShift }, 3);

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.ClickEx(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,gdio.common.objects.Vector2,System.UInt64,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to perform in-game mouse-clicks combined with key press operations. The total frame count of this operation is clickFrameCount + keysNumberOfFrames + modifiersNumberOfFrames
member.param: The mouse button to use for the click operation. See .
The position to perform the mouse click.
The number of frames to click the specific position.
An array of keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.
An array of modifier keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the modifier keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.

Total time in milliseconds to wait after pressing modifier keys, before clicking the object. This is needed when a delay is required to register the modifier keys have been pressed in game. If this delay is longer than that of the frame count for all of the key presses and click operations, the resulting behavior may not be what the user intends.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the ClickEx request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.
member.returns: true if the click was performed successfully; false on timeout or failure.

example.code: //Clicks the left mouse button at 0,0 for 30 frames while holding the left-shift key for 3 frames, and the C key for 5 frames. api.ClickEx(MouseButtons.LEFT, new Vector2(0, 0), 30, new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.C }, 5, new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.LeftShift }, 3, 500, 30);

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.ClickEx(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,System.Single,System.Single,System.UInt64,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to perform in game mouse-clicks combined with key press operations. The total frame count execution of this operation is clickFrameCount + keysNumberOfFrames + modifiersNumberOfFrames.
member.param: The mouse button to use for the click operation. See .
The X coordinate of the game to click in Screen space.
The Y coordinate of the game to click in Screen space.
The number of frames to click the specific position.
An array of keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.
An array of modifier keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the modifier keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.

Total time in milliseconds to wait after pressing modifier keys, before clicking the object. This is needed when a delay is required to register the modifier keys have been pressed in the game. If this delay is longer than that of the frame count for all of the key presses and click operations, the resulting behavior may not be what the user intends.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the ClickEx request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.
member.returns: true if the click was performed successfully; false on timeout or failure.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.ClickObject(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,System.String,System.UInt64,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to interact with an in-game object using mouse-clicks.
member.param: The mouse button to use for the click operation. See .
The HierarchyPath for the GameObject to perform a click on.
The number of frames to click the specific object.
The HierarchyPath to the Camera rendering the object. An empty string uses Camera.main

The number of frames to hold the keys down before clicking. Total press frame count is keyFrames + frames. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.
member.returns: true if the click was performed successfully; false on timeout or failure.

example.code: //Locates and clicks the first object with the name "Cube" for 30 frames using the left mouse button. api.ClickObject(MouseButtons.LEFT, "//*[@name='Cube']", 30, "", 30); // Using the default camera to locate the object. api.ClickObject(MouseButtons.LEFT, "//*[@name='Cube']", 30, "//*[@name='AlternateCamera']/fn:component('UnityEngine.Camera')", 30); // Using AlternateCamera to locate the button. api.ClickObject(MouseButtons.LEFT, "//*[@name='UIDocument']/fn:component('UnityEngine.UIElements.UIDocument')/@rootVisualElement/fn:q('button-1')", 10); // Example usage with a UIToolkit button.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.ClickObjectEx(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,System.String,System.UInt64,System.String,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to interact with an in-game object using mouse-clicks combined with key press operations. The total frame count execution of this operation is clickFrameCount + keysNumberOfFrames + modifiersNumberOfFrames.
member.param: The mouse button to use for the click operation. See .
The HierarchyPath for the GameObject to perform a click on.
The number of frames to click the specific object.
The HierarchyPath to the Camera rendering the object. An empty string uses Camera.main
An array of keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.
An array of modifier keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the modifier keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.

Total time in milliseconds to wait after pressing modifier keys, before clicking the object. This is needed when a delay is required to register the modifier keys have been pressed in the game. If this delay is longer than that of the frame count for all of the key presses and click operations, the resulting behavior may not be what the user intends.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the ClickObjectEx request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.
member.returns: true if the click was performed successfully; false on timeout or failure.

example.code: //Locates and clicks the first object with the name "Cube" for 30 frames with the left mouse button while holding the left-shift key for 3 frames, and the left-CTRL key for 5 frames. api.ClickObjectEx(MouseButtons.LEFT, "//*[@name='Cube']", 30, "", new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.LeftControl }, 5, new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.LeftShift }, 3, 500, 30); api.ClickObjectEx(MouseButtons.LEFT, "//*[@name='Cube']", 30, "//*[@name='AlternateCamera']/fn:component('UnityEngine.Camera')", new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.LeftControl }, 5, new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.LeftShift }, 3, 500, 30); // Using AlternateCamera to locate the button.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.ClickObjectEx(System.String,gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,System.UInt64,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to interact with an in-game object or UI items using mouse-clicks combined with modifier key press operations. The total frame count execution of this operation is clickFrameCount + modifiersNumberOfFrames.
member.param: The mouse button to use for the click operation. See .
The HierarchyPath for the GameObject to perform a click on.
The number of frames to click the specific object.
An array of modifier keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the modifier keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.
The HierarchyPath to the Camera rendering the object. An empty string uses Camera.main
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the ClickObjectEx request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.
member.returns: true if the click was performed successfully; false on timeout or failure.

example.code: //Locates and clicks the first object with the name "Cube" for 10 frames with the left mouse button while holding the left-shift key for 3 frames longer. api.ClickObjectEx(MouseButtons.LEFT, "//*[@name='Cube']", 10, new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.LeftShift }, 3); api.ClickObjectEx(MouseButtons.LEFT, "//*[@name='Cube']", 10, new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.LeftShift }, 3, "//*[@name='AlternateCamera']/fn:component('UnityEngine.Camera')", 30); // Using AlternateCamera to locate the button. api.ClickObjectEx(MouseButtons.LEFT, "//*[@name='UIDocument']/fn:component('UnityEngine.UIElements.UIDocument')/@rootVisualElement/fn:q('button-1')", 10, new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.LeftShift }); // Example usage with a UIToolkit button.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.UseWebSockets(System.String,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Configure a WebSocket server for a client (GDIOAgent) to connect to.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.ConfigureInfoBox(System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Int32,gdio.common.objects.Vector3,gdio.common.objects.Vector2,System.Int32)
member.summary: Updates the configuration of the infobox. Can be used to hide/unhide it and change position and scale.
member.param: Whether the infobox should be shown or not.
True to togle the dropdown of the infobox, false to leave it alone. This is a toggle, not a true/false for show not show.
The render mode of the canvas. 0 = Screen Space Overlay, 1 = Screen Space Camera, 2 = World Space.
The rect position of the infobox.
Rhe scale of the infobox.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the ConfigureInfoBox request was processed.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.Connect(System.String,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Use this function to connect to a Unity game with GameDriver Agent configured and active. This function can connect to the Unity editor or a Standalone deployment of a game.
member.param: The hostname of the machine running the game.
The configured port that the GameDriver agent is configured to use.
Autoplay allows you to automatically start a game in the Unity editor without manual intervention.
The amount of time in seconds to wait for connectivity to establish with the game.
Auto port resolution allows for the Unity editor or Standalone game to report what port it is using for the GameDriver agent.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.CreateInputDevice(System.String,System.String,System.String[],System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to create a device of any type from a Unity layout (OculusHMD, Mouse, etc).
member.param: The type of device to be created, according to a layout.

The custom name for the device. Duplicated names will only return the already existing device. This MUST be different from deviceType. Ex: deviceType OculusHMD, deviceName OculusVirtualHMD or VirtualHMD, etc and NOT OculusHMD

Tags attached to this device, used in finding a path to it. Tags "Gamepad" should be added for gamepads and "LeftHand" or "RightHand" for VR controllers. Failure to add tags for these device types may result in an inability to simulate input.
Whether the name will have GDIO appended to it at the beginning. Note that if using the recorder, this should be false.
The amount of time in seconds to wait for connectivity to establish with the game.

example.code: // Creates an Oculus headset device with the name "VirtualOculusHMD" api.CreateInputDevice("OculusHMD", "VirtualOculusHMD");

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.CreateInputDevice(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean,System.String[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to create a device of any type from a Unity layout (OculusHMD, Mouse, etc).
member.param: The type of device to be created, according to a layout. For Mouse, KB and Touchscreen it can also be the full Type name such as UnityEngine.InputSystem.Mouse

The custom name for the device. If the device already exists, the same device will be used. This MUST be different from deviceType. Ex: deviceType OculusHMD, deviceName OculusVirtualHMD or VirtualHMD, etc and NOT OculusHMD
Setting this to true will disable any default Input device of this type, if present, preventing any interfering inputs.

Tags attached to this device, used in finding a path to it. Tags "Gamepad" should be added for gamepads and "LeftHand" or "RightHand" for VR controllers. Failure to add tags for these device types may result in no input being sent.
The amount of time in seconds to wait for connectivity to establish with the game.
member.returns: The Input path for the device

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.LoadDeviceDescription(System.String)
member.summary: Loads a controller description file. Can be obtained in Unity in the Input Debug by right clicking a device and selecting "Copy Device Description" then pasting to a .json file
member.param: Path of a Json description file. Should be relative to the test folder.

example.code: // Loads a device description file api.LoadDeviceDescription(@"/folderpath/filename.json");

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.CreateInputDeviceFromDescription(System.String,System.String,System.String[],System.Boolean,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to create a device from a controller's description file. Use "LoadDeviceDescription" to obtain the jsonDescription string, or create your own.
member.param: The Json controller description loaded into a string
The custom name for the device. Duplicated names will only return the already existing device.

Tags attached to this device, used in finding a path to it. Tags "Gamepad" should be added for gamepads and "LeftHand" or "RightHand" for VR controllers. These should come last if using the two below. GDIOInputProvider as the first param will instead create or override GDIOs legacy XR devices. Steam can be used as the second param, after GDIOInputProvider, to initialize devices for SteamVR.
Whether the name will have GDIO appended to it at the beginning. Note that if using the recorder, this should be false.

The name of the base this layout is based on. This is a fallback in case the controller is not supported. Use "Gamepad", "OculusHMD", "OculusController", "XRHMD", "XRController" or "TrackedDevice" depending on the device In Unity, other layout names from the layout list can be tested if you know more about them
The amount of time in seconds to wait for connectivity to establish with the game.

example.code: // Creates a device from description api.CreateInputDeviceFromDescription(description, "PS5Controller");

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.RemoveInputDevices(System.Int32)
member.summary: Removes all devices created by GDIO "CreateDevice" methods. Should be used in OneTimeTearDown to prevent issues with controllers remaining in the scene.
member.param: The amount of time in seconds to wait for connectivity to establish with the game.

example.code: // Removes all devices and layouts created by GDIO "CreateDevice" methods. api.RemoveInputDevices();

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.LogMessageOnAgent(gdio.common.objects.LogLevel,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Send a message to be logged by the Agent using a specified log level.
member.param: The logging level for the Agent to use for the message. If this level is [Greater-Than] the current value, it will not be shown in the log.
The logging message to send to the Agent
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the SetInfoBoxStatusMessage request was processed.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.Connect(System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Use this function to connect to a Unity game with GameDriver Agent configured and active. This function can connect to the Unity editor or a Standalone deployment of a game.
member.param: The hostname of the machine running the game.
This parameter is a regular expression that will attempt to match a specfic game via its Application.DataPath if you have multiple games running.
The configured port that the GameDriver agent is configured to use.
Autoplay allows you to automatically start a game in the Unity editor without manual intervention.
The amount of time in seconds to wait for connectivity to establish with the game.
Auto port resolution allows for the Unity editor or Standalone game to report what port it is using for the GameDriver agent.

The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.DoubleClick(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,gdio.common.objects.Vector2,System.UInt64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to perform in-game mouse double-clicks.
member.param: The mouse button to use for the double click operation. See .
The Vector2 location to double click in Screen space.
The number of frames to double click the specific position.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the Click request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.

example.code: //Double-clicks the left mouse button at position 0, 0 on the screen over a duration of 30 frames. api.DoubleClick(MouseButtons.LEFT, new Vector2(0, 0), 30, 30);

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.DoubleClick(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,System.Single,System.Single,System.UInt64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to perform in-game mouse double-clicks.
member.param: The mouse button to use for the double click operation. See .
The X coordinate of the game to double click in Screen space.
The Y coordinate of the game to double click in Screen space.
The number of frames to click the specific location.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the Click request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.

example.code: //Double-clicks the left mouse button at position 0, 0 on the screen over a duration of 30 frames. api.DoubleClick(MouseButtons.LEFT, 0, 0, 30, 30);

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.DoubleClickEx(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,gdio.common.objects.Vector2,System.UInt64,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to perform in game mouse double clicks combined with key press operations. The total frame count execution of this operation is clickFrameCount + keysNumberOfFrames + modifiersNumberOfFrames
member.param: The mouse button to use for the click operation. See .
The Vector2 position of where to double click in Screen space.
The number of frames to click the specific position.
An array of keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.
An array of modifier keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the modifier keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.

Total time in milliseconds to wait after pressing modifier keys, before clicking the object. This is needed when a delay is required to register the modifier keys have been pressed in the game. If this delay is longer than the frame count for all the key presses and click operation that the behavior of the result may not be what the user intends.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the ClickEx request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.DoubleClickEx(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,System.Single,System.Single,System.UInt64,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to perform in game mouse double clicks combined with key press operations. The total frame count execution of this operation is clickFrameCount + keysNumberOfFrames + modifiersNumberOfFrames
member.param: The mouse button to use for the click operation. See .
The X coordinate of the game to double click in Screen space.
The Y coordinate of the game to double click in Screen space.
The number of frames to click the specific position.
An array of keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.
An array of modifier keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the modifier keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.

Total time in milliseconds to wait after pressing modifier keys, before clicking the object. This is needed when a delay is required to register the modifier keys have been pressed in the game. If this delay is longer than the frame count for all the key presses and click operation that the behavior of the result may not be what the user intends.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the ClickEx request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.DoubleClickObject(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,System.String,System.UInt64,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to interact with an object in game using a mouse double-click.
member.param: The mouse button to use for the double click operation. See .
The HierarchyPath for the GameObject to perform a double click on.
The number of frames to double-click the specific object.
The HierarchyPath to the Camera rendering the object. An empty string uses Camera.main
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the DoubleClickObject request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

example.code: //Locates and double-clicks the first object with the name "Cube" over a period of 30 frames using the left mouse button. api.DoubleClickObject(MouseButtons.LEFT, "//*[@name='Cube']", 30, "", 30); // Using the default camera to locate the object. api.DoubleClickObject(MouseButtons.LEFT, "//*[@name='Cube']", 30, "//*[@name='AlternateCamera']/fn:component('UnityEngine.Camera')", 30); // Using AlternateCamera to locate the object.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.DoubleClickObjectEx(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,System.String,System.UInt64,System.String,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to interact with an object in game using a mouse double-click, combinated with key press operations. The total frame count execution of this operation is clickFrameCount + keysNumberOfFrames + modifiersNumberOfFrames
member.param: The mouse button to use for the double click operation. See .
The HierarchyPath for the GameObject to perform a double-click on.
The number of frames to double-click the specific object.
The HierarchyPath to the Camera rendering the object. An empty string uses Camera.main
An array of keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.
An array of modifier keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the modifier keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.

Total time in milliseconds to wait after pressing modifier keys, before clicking the object. This is needed when a delay is required to register the modifier keys have been pressed in the game. If this delay is longer than the frame count for all the key presses and click operation that the behavior of the result may not be what the user intends.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the DoubleClickObjectEx request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

example.code: //Locates and clicks the first object with the name "Cube" for 30 frames with the left mouse button while holding the left-shift key for 3 frames, and the left-CTRL key for 5 frames. api.DoubleClickObjectEx(MouseButtons.LEFT, "//*[@name='Cube']", 30, "", new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.LeftControl }, 5, new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.LeftShift }, 3, 500, 30); // Using the default camera to locate the object api.DoubleClickObjectEx(MouseButtons.LEFT, "//*[@name='Cube']", 30, "//*[@name='AlternateCamera']/fn:component('UnityEngine.Camera')", new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.LeftControl }, 5, new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.LeftShift }, 3, 500, 30); // Using AlternateCamera to locate the object

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.EnableObjectCaching(System.Int32)
member.summary: Enable the use of object caching when performing HierarchyPath object resolution. Object caching is per HierarchyPath stored in a [System.Collections.IDictionary](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.collections.idictionary). If a matching HierarchyPath is already in the dictionary, then the stored object is returned. The only way to update a cached reference is for the reference to be garbage collected or flush the cache with .
member.param: The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

example.code: api.EnableObjectCaching();

member.param: The type of input hook to enable. See
The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

example.code: api.EnableHooks(HookingObject.ALL);

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.GetPropertyList(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Returns a dictionary of the properties and fields attached to the target object.
The key of the dictionary is the hierarchy path of the property.
The value of the dictionary is the value of the property represented as a string.
member.param: The object to get the properties of
The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.
member.returns: A HashSet of gdio.common.object.PropertyInfo

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.GetPropertyTree(System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns a tree of the properties and fields attached to the target object, expanding struct/value-type fields recursively up to levels deep. Class/object-reference fields are returned as typed leaf nodes without recursion. Use the HierarchyPath values in the result to construct chained /@field paths for GetObjectFieldValue.
member.param: The object to inspect.
How many levels of struct nesting to expand. Default: 3.
Timeout in seconds. Default: 30.
member.returns: A HashSet of PropertyInfo where struct nodes have their Children populated.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.GetLoggingLevel(System.Int32)
member.summary: Get the Agent's (remote) current Log Level.
member.param: The number of seconds to wait for a response that the SetInfoBoxStatusMessage request was processed.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.GetMethodList(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns a HashSet of MethodInfo objects representing all methods attached to the target object. Each MethodInfo contains the method name, return type, parameter information, and hierarchy path.
member.param: The HierarchyPath to the object whose methods you want to retrieve.
The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent. Default is 30 seconds.
member.returns: HashSet<gdio.common.objects.MethodInfo> - A collection of MethodInfo objects, each containing method metadata including name, return type, parameters, and parent path.
member.remarks:
This method is consistent across both Unity and Unreal engines, both returning HashSet<MethodInfo>. The MethodInfo objects provide structured metadata about each method, making it easier to: - Discover available methods on game objects - Inspect method signatures before calling them - Filter methods by return type or parameter count - Build dynamic test logic based on available methods
Use the returned MethodInfo data with api.CallMethod() or api.CallMethod<T>() to invoke the methods.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.GetVersionsString
member.summary: Returns the GameDriver component versions in this build.
member.returns: A string containing the GameDriver component version.

example.code: // Log the current GameDriver component version. Console.WriteLine("GameDriver Version: " + api.GetVersionString());

Command line arguments to pass to the executable.

member.summary: This method returns all the control input paths being used.

member.returns: The Debug string (DEBUG and MULTI) or Json string (JSON).

example.code: // Maps the control paths to debug. api.MapInputControlPathsUsed(Cmd_MapInputControlPathsUsedRequest.OutputTypes.DEBUG);

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.MouseDrag(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,gdio.common.objects.Vector2,System.UInt64,gdio.common.objects.Vector2,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.param: The mouse button, , to perform the drag operation with.
The destination vector to drag the mouse to.
The number of frames taken to complete the drag operation.
The origin to start the drag operation. If null, the mouse drag operation will begin at the current position of Input.mousePosition.
Camera hierarchy path if the coordinates are on a secondary display
Wait for the empty input event to be returned from the agent before returning from the method call.

The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent. If waitForEmptyInput is set to TRUE, then the method call will wait the timeout allotment to receive the event.
member.returns: TRUE if the requests was successfully processed by the GameDriver agent.

example.code: // Drags the left mouse button to the position 180, 0 on the screen over 30 frames api.MouseDrag(MouseButtons.LEFT, new Vector2(180, 0), 30, start, true);

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.MouseMoveToObject(System.String,System.UInt64,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Move the mouse to the center of a specific object, identified by the HierarchyPath.
member.param: The HierarchyPath to move the mouse pointer to.
The number of frames to complete the mouse move over.
The HierarchyPath to the camera object.
If TRUE, wait for the object to exist so that mouse move can complete successfully.
Wait for the empty input event to be returned from the GameDriver agent, before returning from the method.

The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent. If waitForEmptyInput is TRUE, then the method call will wait the timeout allotment to receive the event.
member.returns: TRUE if the method call was successfully processed by the GameDriver agent.

example.code: // Moves the mouse to the center of the object named "Cylinder" over 300 frames api.MouseMoveToObject("//*[@name='Cylinder']", 300, true, true);

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.MouseMoveToPoint(gdio.common.objects.Vector2,System.UInt64,gdio.common.objects.Vector2,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Move the mouse to the destination vector.
member.param: Destination vector to move the mouse to.
The number of frames to complete the operation over.
The origin to start the mouse move operation. If null, the mouse move operation will begin at the current position of Input.mousePosition.
Camera hierarchy path if the coordinates to move to is on a secondary display
Wait for the empty input event to be returned from the GameDriver agent, before returning from the method.

The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent. If waitForEmptyInput is TRUE, then the method call will wait the timeout allotment to receive the event.
member.returns: TRUE if the method call was successfully processed by the GameDriver agent.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.NavAgentMoveToPoint(System.String,gdio.common.objects.Vector3,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Move a NavAgent to a destination point.

The destination vector to move to.
Wait for the NavAgent move-to operation to complete before the method returns.
The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.

example.code: // Moves the 1st object named "Ellen" to position 20, 0, 20 on the NavMesh api.NavAgentMoveToPoint("//*[@name='Ellen']", new Vector3(20, 0, 20));

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.ObjectExists(System.String)
member.summary: Check for an object to exist. Do NOT use this to poll, use WaitForObject instead. There is a builtin response timeout of 5 seconds if the Agent/Game does not respond.
member.param: The HierarchyPath of the object.
member.returns: TRUE if the object exists.

example.code: api.ObjectExists("//*[@name='Cube']"); //Checks if an object named "Cube" to exist

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.PoseInputEvent(System.String,gdio.common.objects.Vector3,gdio.common.objects.Quaternion,System.UInt64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to send arbitrary combinations of Vector3 and Quaternions to a game. This is usually the way when dealing with matrices. This is only used by SteamVR for now.
member.param: The Input Control Path for the New Input System
Value of the Vector3 value of the event
Value of the Quaternion value of the event
The number of frames to press and hold the axis for.
The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

example.code: api.PoseInputEvent("actions/default/in/Pose", new Vector3(0, 0, 5), api.EulerToQuat(0.0f, 90.0f, 0.0f), 2); // Set a SteamVR action position and rotation.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.QuaternionInputEvent(System.String,gdio.common.objects.Quaternion,System.UInt64,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Use this function to send arbitrary Quaternion Event states to the game.

Value of the Quaternion event
The number of frames to press and hold the axis for.
The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.
Force the input to go to legacy systems or not.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

example.code: api.QuaternionInputEvent("OculusLeftHand/deviceRotation", api.EulerToQuat(-10, 0, 0), 5); //Turn controller by -10 degrees around its x-axis.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.Raycast(gdio.common.objects.Vector3,System.String,System.Int32)

The HierarchyPath for the UnityEngine.Camera to use as a Raycast reference. If the Camera is string.Empty, the Camera.main is used
The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.
member.returns: Returns an array of .

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.ReconfigurePort(System.Int32,System.Int32)

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.RegisterCollisionMonitor(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Register a collision monitor to recieve collision events on an object.
member.param: The HierarchyPath for the object to register a collection detection monitor.
The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.
member.returns: Returns a String representing the event identifier

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.RotateObject(System.String,gdio.common.objects.Quaternion,System.Boolean,System.Int32)

The quaternion value to manipulate the object.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.RotateObject(System.String,gdio.common.objects.Vector3,gdio.common.objects.Space,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Rotate an object defined by the HierarchyPath and rotated by Eulers. for more information.
member.param: The HierarchyPath for the object to rotate.
The Euler's vector to manipulate the object.
The to perform the rotation relative to.
Wait for the object to exist if it doesn't.
The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.
member.returns: TRUE if the method call was successfully processed by the GameDriver agent.

example.code: // Rotates an object with the name "Cylinder" by 30 degrees on the y-axis. api.RotateObject("//*[@name='Cylinder']", new Vector3(0, 30, 0), Space.Self, true);

The Y angle value to rotate.
The Z angle value to rotate.
The to perform the rotation relative to.
Wait for the object to exist if it doesn't.
The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.
member.returns: TRUE if the method call was successfully processed by the GameDriver agent.

example.code: // Rotates an object with the name "Cylinder" by 20 degrees on the z axis api.RotateObject("//*[@name='Cylinder']", 0, 0, 20);

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.RotateObject(System.String,gdio.common.objects.Vector3,System.Single,gdio.common.objects.Space,System.Boolean,System.Int32)

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.Scroll(System.Single,System.Single,System.UInt64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to simulate mouse wheel scrolling. NOTE: The new input system values are a factor of 120
member.param: The x value of the scroll vector for the mouse, usually 0.
The y value of the scroll vector for the mouse, typically -1 (-120) <= y <= +1 (120)
The number of frames to activate scroll event.
The time to wait for the agent to process the Scroll request.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent was able to successfully process the request.

example.code: // Scrolls from 0 to 10 over 100 frames api.Scroll(0, 0, 100);

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.SetInputFieldText(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Set the text of an InputField or TMP_InputField
member.param: The HierarchyPath to the InputField or TMP_InputField.
The text value to set in the field.
If TRUE, wait for the object to exist if it doesn't.
The time to wait for the object to exist.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent was able to successfully set the InputField text.

example.code: //Sets the text field component attached to the "TextMeshPro InputField" object with the tag "FilterInputTxt" to the value of "America" api.SetInputFieldText("//FilterInputTxt[@name='TextMeshPro InputField']", "America");

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.SetXRDeviceMode(System.String,gdio.common.objects.XRDeviceModes)
member.summary: Used to change between controller and hand modes in XR if one of the used modes is not working or being rendered. Mostly for OVR. Set by default to both: XRDeviceModes.CONTROLLER | XRDeviceModes.HAND. Ex: SetXRDeviceModes("GDIOLeftHand", XRDeviceModes.HAND) or SetXRDeviceModes("GDIOLeftHand", XRDeviceModes.CONTROLLER | XRDeviceModes.HAND).
member.param: The name of the device to switch mode.
The mode of the device to switch into.
member.returns: True if the device is found and the mode is switched, an exception and false otherwise.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.StopEditorPlay
member.summary: Stop a game from playing in the Editor, if it is currently in Play mode.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.SetObjectFieldValue(System.Object,System.String,System.Object,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Set the field or property of an object.
member.param: The HierarchyPath to the object to set the field/property value. This can be a System.String or an ObjectRef
The field or property name to set the value of.
The value to set for the field/property.
If TRUE, wait for the object to exist if it doesn't.
The time to wait for the object to exist.
member.returns: TRUE if the field/property was successfully set to the value.

example.code: // Sets the text field component attached to the "TextMeshPro InputField" object with the tag "FilterInputTxt" to the value of "Asia" api.SetObjectFieldValue("/Untagged[@name='Canvas']/FilterInputTxt[@name='TextMeshPro InputField']/fn:component('TMPro.TMP_InputField')", "text", "string:Asia");

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.SetInfoBoxStatusMessage(System.String[],System.Int32)

The number of seconds to wait for a response that the SetInfoBoxStatusMessage request was processed.

The number of seconds to wait for a response that the SetInfoBoxStatusMessage request was processed.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.WaitForCollisionEvent(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Wait for a collision event to fire on an element that is being monitored for collisions. If the method has been called before, there is the potential that another event was recieved before waiting on the event again. Check with to see if it returns null to see if an event was missed.
member.param: The eventId received from the call.
The number of seconds to wait for a collision event to be fired.
member.returns: The contains information about the collision. NULL is returned if the request times out.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.WaitForEmptyInput(System.Int32)
member.summary: Wait for an Empty Input event to be received. NOTE: Some methods have an internal WaitForEmptyInput parameter, do not use this with them.
member.param: The time to wait for the event in seconds.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.WaitForEmptyInput(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Wait for an Empty Input event in a specific path to be received. NOTE: Some methods have an internal WaitForEmptyInput parameter, do not use this with them.
member.param: The input path to wait for.
The time to wait for the event in seconds.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.WaitForObject(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Wait for an object to exist.
member.param: The HierarchyPath of the object.
The amount of time to wait for the object to exist in seconds.
member.returns: TRUE if the object exists within the allotted timeout.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.ApiClient.WaitForObjectValue(System.String,System.String,System.Object,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Wait for an object to exist and have a specific value for a specified field/property.

The field/property name for inspection.
The value to wait for.
If TRUE, wait for the object to exist if it doesn't.
The amount of time to wait for the object to exist with the specified value in seconds.
member.returns: TRUE if the object exists with the specified value in the allotted timeout.

example.code: //Waits for the value of the TextMeshProUGUI input field with the name "Text" to be "America" api.WaitForObjectValue("//*[@name='Text']/fn:component('TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI')", "text", "America");

member.name: E:gdio.unity_api.v2.CoApiClient.LoggedMessage

member.name: E:gdio.unity_api.v2.CoApiClient.ScriptSignal

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.CoApiClient.Click(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,gdio.common.objects.Vector2,System.UInt64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to perform in-game mouse-clicks.
member.param: The mouse button to use for the click operation. See .
The position to perform the mouse click.
The number of frames to click the specific position.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the Click request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.

example.code: api.Click(MouseButtons.LEFT, new Vector2 (0, 0), 30, 30); //Clicks the left mouse button at 0,0 for 30 frames

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.CoApiClient.Click(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,System.Single,System.Single,System.UInt64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to perform in-game mouse-clicks.
member.param: The mouse button to use for the click operation. See .
The X coordinate of the game to click in Screen space.
The Y coordinate of the game to click in Screen space.
The number of frames to click the specific position.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the Click request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.

example.code: api.Click(MouseButtons.LEFT, 0, 0, 30, 30); //Clicks the left mouse button at 0,0 for 30 frames

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.CoApiClient.ClickEx(gdio.common.objects.MouseButtons,gdio.common.objects.Vector2,System.UInt64,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,gdio.unity_api.KeyCode[],System.UInt64,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to perform in-game mouse-clicks combined with key press operations. The total frame count of this operation is clickFrameCount + keysNumberOfFrames + modifiersNumberOfFrames
member.param: The mouse button to use for the click operation. See .
The position to perform the mouse click.
The number of frames to click the specific position.
An array of keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.
An array of modifier keys to press during the click operation.
The number of frames to press the modifier keys parameter down. This parameter is not additive to the total count and is automatically accumulated by the function call.

Total time in milliseconds to wait after pressing modifier keys, before clicking the object. This is needed when a delay is required to register the modifier keys have been pressed in game. If this delay is longer than that of the frame count for all of the key presses and click operations, the resulting behavior may not be what the user intends.
The number of seconds to wait for a response that the ClickEx request was processed. Input requests process asynchronously, so a response does NOT imply the input operation has completed.

example.code: //Clicks the left mouse button at 0,0 for 30 frames while holding the left-shift key for 3 frames, and the C key for 5 frames. api.ClickEx(MouseButtons.LEFT, new Vector2(0, 0), 30, new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.C }, 5, new KeyCode[] { KeyCode.LeftShift }, 3, 500, 30);

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.CoApiClient.EnableObjectCaching(System.Int32)
member.summary: Enable the use of object caching when performing HierarchyPath object resolution. Object caching is per HierarchyPath stored in a [System.Collections.IDictionary](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.collections.idictionary). If a matching HierarchyPath is already in the dictionary, then the stored object is returned. The only way to update a cached reference is for the reference to be garbage collected or flush the cache with .
member.param: The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.
member.returns: TRUE if the GameDriver agent successfully processed the request.

example.code: api.EnableObjectCaching();

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.CoApiClient.EnableHooks(gdio.unity_api.v2.HookingObject,System.Int32)

The timeout in seconds to wait for a response that the request was processed by the GameDriver agent.

member.name: M:gdio.unity_api.v2.CoApiClient.WaitForCollisionEvent``1(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Wait for a collision event to fire on an element that is being monitored for collisions. If the method has been called before, there is the potential that another event was recieved before waiting on the event again. Check with to see if it returns null to see if an event was missed.
member.param: The eventId received from the call.
The number of seconds to wait for a collision event to be fired.
member.returns: The collision messages.

example.code: api.WaitForCollisionEvent{object}(eventId);
